claim_candidate: A nonlinear model can robustly outperform logistic regression across repeated seeds on a public sensor classification dataset.
falsification_condition: If logistic regression achieves balanced accuracy within 1 percentage point of the best nonlinear model across 10 seeds, or if the effect_to_noise_ratio is less than 1.5.
minimum_evidence_needed: One dataset with 10 repeated seeds, showing consistent advantage for nonlinear models.
why_it_matters: Domain literature shows that sensor data often contains complex interactions and nonlinear patterns that linear models may miss. For example, transformer-based HAR models outperform simpler baselines in heterogeneous environments.
